tasks.description: Enhance GET /v1/invoices with date range, companyId, chain, and amount range filters
tasks.context:
The current GET /v1/invoices only supports status and customerEmail filters. Live users (AI agents) need to query by company, date range, chain, and amount. This is a pure backend enhancement with no schema changes — all filter data is already stored in the Invoice table.

EDIT: backend/src/routes/invoices.ts

In the GET /v1/invoices handler (around line 125), add these new query params AFTER the existing status and customerEmail:

  const companyId = req.query.companyId as string | undefined;
  const chain = req.query.chain as string | undefined;          // filter by paymentDetails.network
  const fromDate = req.query.from as string | undefined;         // ISO date string
  const toDate = req.query.to as string | undefined;             // ISO date string
  const minAmount = req.query.minAmount as string | undefined;   // numeric
  const maxAmount = req.query.maxAmount as string | undefined;   // numeric
  const sortBy = (req.query.sortBy as string) || 'createdAt';    // 'createdAt' | 'amount' | 'updatedAt'
  const sortDir = (req.query.sortDir as string) === 'asc' ? true : false; // ascending default false

And add these filters to the Supabase query chain:
  if (companyId) query = query.eq('companyId', companyId);
  if (fromDate) query = query.gte('createdAt', fromDate);
  if (toDate) query = query.lte('createdAt', toDate);
  if (minAmount) query = query.gte('amount', parseFloat(minAmount));
  if (maxAmount) query = query.lte('amount', parseFloat(maxAmount));

For chain filter: chain is stored in paymentDetails.network (JSON field). Supabase supports JSON path filters:
  if (chain) query = query.contains('paymentDetails', { network: chain.toLowerCase() });

For sorting: replace the hardcoded .order('createdAt', { ascending: false }) with:
  const validSortFields = ['createdAt', 'amount', 'updatedAt'];
  const safeSortBy = validSortFields.includes(sortBy) ? sortBy : 'createdAt';
  query = query.order(safeSortBy, { ascending: sortDir });

Make sure the file stays under 200 lines total. If needed, extract the filter-building logic into a local helper function buildInvoiceFilters().

CREATE: backend/src/routes/__tests__/invoices-enhanced-filters.test.ts

Tests:
1. GET /v1/invoices with companyId filter — Supabase mock returns 2 invoices
2. GET /v1/invoices with fromDate filter — verify gte('createdAt') called
3. GET /v1/invoices with toDate filter — verify lte('createdAt') called
4. GET /v1/invoices with minAmount filter — verify gte('amount') called
5. GET /v1/invoices with chain filter — verify contains('paymentDetails') called
6. GET /v1/invoices with sortBy=amount&sortDir=asc — verify order call
7. GET /v1/invoices with invalid sortBy — defaults to createdAt

Max 150 lines for tests. Use express supertest + mock @supabase/supabase-js.

tasks.description: Add GET /v1/invoices/stats endpoint — invoice totals and revenue metrics by company
Add GET /v1/reputation/leaderboard endpoint — top agents ranked by reputation score
Add unit tests for ledger/enforcement.ts — hierarchical budget enforcement logic
tasks.context:
The last untested major service is backend/src/services/ledger/enforcement.ts (377 lines). It depends on budget.ts functions (mock using jest.mock('../budget')). This is the budget guard for AI agents — any regression would let agents overspend.

First READ enforcement.ts fully to understand all exported functions before writing tests.

CREATE: backend/src/services/ledger/__tests__/enforcement.test.ts (max 200 lines)

Mock strategy:
  jest.mock('../budget', () => ({
    checkBudget: jest.fn(),
    getBudget: jest.fn(),
    createBudgetReservation: jest.fn(),
    cancelBudgetReservation: jest.fn(),
    consumeBudgetReservation: jest.fn(),
  }));

Test targets (read enforcement.ts to find all exports):
1. checkHierarchicalBudget() — allowed when all levels allow
2. checkHierarchicalBudget() — denied when department budget exceeded
3. checkHierarchicalBudget() — denied when team budget exceeded (department OK)
4. checkHierarchicalBudget() — denied when agent budget exceeded (department+team OK)
5. checkHierarchicalBudget() with skipLevels — skips specified BudgetLevel
6. Any other exported functions — read the file and test them

Min: 12 tests. Tests should cover all exported functions.
Mock the `db` argument as an empty object `{} as any` — enforcement.ts passes db to budget.ts functions which are mocked.
